topic: Bunjil's birthday planning — wants a fishing trip

turns.content: Need to plan a birthday for Bunjil. He is turning 11 next week and he does not want a party. He wants a fishing trip. Just him and a couple of mates out on the water. I need to sort the logistics.
A fishing trip sounds like a great birthday plan. To help think it through — where are you considering going, how many kids total, and have they all been out fishing before?

One of the billabongs, not the river. Safer for the kids, calmer water. He wants to bring two mates, so that is three boys and me. They have all been fishing before but they are 10 and 11 year olds so they need watching. I have two proper rods and two hand lines. Life jackets are at the station.

A billabong is a sensible choice — much easier to keep an eye on everyone than a river. Three boys plus you is manageable. A few things worth sorting out:

- Bait: prawns or cut bait would be easiest for the boys on hand lines.
- Shade: is there tree cover at the spot, or do you need to bring a tarp?
- Safety check of the water and banks before the boys set up.
- Food and water — they'll burn through a lot in the heat.

An early start would beat the worst of the sun and catch the fish when they're feeding.

Prawns from the shop in Jabiru. There are big paperbark trees along one side of the billabong so shade is sorted. I know this spot, there is usually a freshie or two but no salties. I will still check before we set up though. Always check. For food I was thinking damper on a fire by the water with honey instead of a birthday cake. That is Bunjil's favourite thing.

Damper with honey by the water sounds like a memorable birthday meal. You could get the coals going while the boys fish in the morning, and have it ready by mid-morning as a break. That gives the trip a nice rhythm — fish, eat, then fish some more. Do you have everything you need for the fire and cooking, or is there anything to pick up?

I have flour and honey at home. Just need to grab the prawns and maybe some extra water bottles. The other thing is a present. He wants a tackle box. Nothing fancy. He is a simple kid. Honestly if he catches a fish that will be the best present for him.

A tackle box is a practical gift — he'll use it every time he goes fishing and think of this birthday. You could pick one up when you're in Jabiru getting the prawns. Have you let the other boys' families know the plan yet?

I will talk to their families this week. They know me so it will be straightforward. One of the boys, his dad might want to come along too. That would be good. More eyes on the water with three boys. And he is a good fisherman so the boys could learn from him.

Another adult would be helpful, especially if the boys spread out along the bank. And having someone else who knows fishing means the boys get more attention. Worth asking him directly — he might be happy to come if he knows it's a small, relaxed trip rather than a big event.
I will ask him when I talk to the family. What about timing. I was thinking leave at first light, fish until about 11, then head back before it gets too hot. The boys will be tired by then anyway.

That's a good window. First light to 11 gives you about five hours, which is plenty for that age group. The fish will be most active early, and by mid-morning the boys will be getting restless anyway. Wrapping up before the heat peaks means everyone has a good time without anyone getting sunburnt or overheated. Make sure they all have hats and sunscreen on from the start.

Hats and sunscreen, yes. I will remind the families to send them with hats. Bunjil is proper excited about it. He has been talking about what fish he is going to catch for days. Keeps checking the rods to make sure they are ready.

That kind of anticipation is half the fun of a birthday at that age. Sounds like you've got everything covered — prawns and tackle box from Jabiru, damper supplies at home, early start, back by 11. I hope the fish cooperate and he lands a good one.
If he catches a barra on his birthday he will not stop talking about it for a year. Even a catfish would make him happy though. Thanks for helping me think it through.
Any fish on your birthday is a good fish. Hope it's a great day for him and the boys. Enjoy it.
